Ultra Clean Holdings Inc expected to post earnings of 27 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:

data.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Technology, Media and Telecom. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1105 ET - Charter Communications logged lower-than-expected earnings in 2Q, while revenue was broadly in-line with Wall Street forecasts. Charter is plunging in early trading because investors are likely keying on "just nominally positive revenues and Ebitda growth, and a higher-than-expected (117K) broadband unit loss," Benchmark says in a research note. Analyst Matthew Harrigan believes the reaction is excessive. On the positive side, he points to an 80% year-over-year improvement in video losses to just 80K from 408K in 2Q24. He also mentions a $500 million reduction to the company's capital expenditures guide to $11.5 billion and 500K Spectrum Mobile unit growth, which he says was in-line. Charter slides 17%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1003 ET - Imax reported better-than-expected 2Q results buoyed by rising demand and market share gains. As theaters upgrade to premium screens and more films are shot using Imax technology, the company is well-positioned for continued growth, Wedbush analysts say in a research note. They expect momentum to build into next year, fueled by a strong slate of Imax-formatted releases. Imax is also diversifying beyond Hollywood, expanding into local-language films, global content and alternative programming, while continuing to grow its international footprint. Imax is off 2.2% in early trading. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

0927 ET - Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan says the company is developing its advanced 14A technology, which will allow it to make more efficient chips. However, Tan also says on a call with analysts that 14A won't ramp until near the end of the decade. The timeline, he adds, is dependent on Intel securing "a meaningful external customer" to support development costs. Customer decisions on 14A node adoption likely won't be made for another 18-24 months, JPMorgan analysts say in a research note. As a result, they say there is near-term uncertainty surrounding Intel's foundry and AI roadmap. And "in the meantime, Intel will continue to burn cash and concede more market share in the PC client and server business to AMD," the analysts say. Intel drops 8% premarket. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

0911 ET - Intel delivered 2Q results that were relatively in-line with expectations, according to Benchmark Equity Research, but the chipmaker's 3Q outlook was mixed, and investors are worried about the uncertainty surrounding its advanced 14A technology, analyst Cody Acree says in a research note. "We expect it will likely be at least several quarters, if not years, before investors can realistically expect to see material changes in Intel's design or manufacturing competitiveness, its market share, its foundry business, or its broader financial picture," Acree says. Intel sinks 8% premarket. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

0819 ET - Nokia's momentum in its network infrastructure business is key for UBS to become more constructive on the shares, given headwinds in the mobile networks business, analyst Francois-Xavier Bouvignies writes. Second-quarter results brought limited incremental news, after the prerelease earlier in the week, where the Finnish telecom-equipment provider cut its 2025 guidance. Currency and, to a lesser extent, tariffs are offsetting the cyclical recovery in the network infrastructure and cloud-and-networks units, UBS says. The bank forecasts a solid second half of the year for these businesses, outweighed by the macro headwinds and muted growth in mobile networks. "Despite the clear value argument, we see limited catalysts to drive the shares higher and remain concerned around structural risks longer terms." Shares fall 2.4%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0622 ET - Hesai Group is likely to benefit from the rapid expansion of China's robotaxi fleet, leveraging its solid position in the robotaxi LiDAR market, Daiwa analysts write in a note. Hesai may maintain robust LiDAR delivery momentum and reach breakeven in 2Q, they add. The company has long dominated the global robotaxi LiDAR market thanks to the performance and cost advantages of its mechanical LiDAR. Chinese robotaxi operators began large-scale commercialization of their services in 2025, cutting costs by adopting semi-solid-state LiDAR to replace more expensive mechanical versions, an area where Hesai again holds a competitive edge, they say. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0556 ET - Jefferies turns more positive on Kuaishou Technology after updates on Kling, its AI video generation tool. The firm's latest update in June said that Kling AI's monthly subscription bookings exceeded 100 million yuan in both April and May this year. Kling is globally top ranked among large video generation models and independent AI products by both revenue growth and overall revenue scale, Jefferies analysts note. As it accelerates monetization, the addressable market is huge for video content production, reaching about US$120 billion globally, they say. Jefferies maintains a buy call on Kuaishou and raises its target price to HK$92.00 from HK$81.00. Shares last ended at HK$72.50. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0322 ET - Coforge has a promising outlook for its 2Q FY 2026 earnings, based on management commentary, Elara Securities (India) analysts say in a research report. The Indian IT services provider's deal with Sabre Corp. is likely to generate meaningful contribution from 2Q, the analysts say. Coforge also maintains its revenue target for FY 2027 of US$2 billion, with medium-term growth expected to be broad-based. Coforge continues to benefit from its strengths such as robust execution capability. The brokerage boosts its FY 2026-2027 revenue estimates by 4%-5% and raises the stock's target price to INR1,850.00 from INR1,770.00 with an unchanged accumulate rating. Shares are 1.0% higher at INR1,692.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2234 ET - LG Display could post a strong profit turnaround in 2H, Nomura analysts Eon Hwang and Heesoo Min write in a note. The South Korean flat-screen maker's organic light-emitting-diode TV panel business stands to benefit from a market recovery and lower depreciation costs, they say. Its OLED mobile panel business also stands to benefit from easing supply competition for iPhone 17 products, they note. The analysts expect LG to report an operating profit of 451 billion won in 3Q and 680 billion won in 4Q, following its 116 billion won loss in 2Q. Nomura raises its 2025 net-profit forecast for LG by 74% to 1.232 trillion won. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Health Care Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Health Care sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1009 ET - Centene CEO Sarah London says its Medicaid portfolio fell short of expectations in 2Q. London on an earnings call adds the Medicaid portfolio produced an "unanticipated and unacceptable health benefits ratio of 94.9%." The managed-care specialist attributed the underperformance to a step up in medical costs in three areas: behavioral health, home health and high-cost drugs. London says behavioral health was the most significant driver of the quarter-over-quarter increase with applied behavioral analysis as an accelerating pressure point across a number of its markets. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

0936 ET — Centene says an analysis of data from an independent actuarial firm confirmed a significant shift in the marketplace risk pool in 2025, which it sees being primarily driven by three things. CEO Sarah London on a 2Q earnings call notes that a higher-than-expected percentage of healthy and/or low-utilizing members left the marketplace during open enrollment. Additionally, new signups to the market had higher morbidity, likely reflecting changes in the underlying member mix, London adds. Lastly, a step up in marketplace utilization more broadly, combined with more aggressive provider coding, is likely driving higher-in-the-year documented morbidity, the CEO concludes. The managed-care specialist now expects 2025 earnings to be pressured by $2.4 billion, up from an earlier estimate of $1.8 billion. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

0929 ET — Centene now expects 2025 earnings to be pressured by $2.4 billion, up from an earlier estimate of $1.8 billion. The managed-care specialist in July withdrew its guidance for the year and disclosed that it has data showing that enrollees in the marketplaces overall appeared to be fewer, and less healthy, than expected. As a result, it expects to get a smaller payout from a federal program designed to even out risk across insurers. Shares rise 3.5% to $27.90 in premarket trading despite a loss in 2Q and declines in membership for certain health insurance lines. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

0445 ET - China's pharmaceutical sector is likely to sustain its strong momentum, HSBC analysts say in a research note. The sector has outperformed the market year-to-date, with the Hang Seng Healthcare Index up more than 70%, driven by breakthroughs in business development such as 3S Bio's licensing deals with Pfizer, they note. A further sector re-rating likely after a robust 1H performance, they say. HSBC maintains buy ratings on leading players including Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals, Sino Biopharmaceutical and Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0414 ET - China's plan to rectify a price war in the pharma industry should bode well for the sector, DBS says in a note. In future selections of medical-products suppliers for public hospitals, the lowest bid might not win, China's National Healthcare Security Administration says. DBS thinks this move alleviates the largest risk to China's pharma sector--price cuts. Chinese pharma stocks could have further room to grow as the valuation isn't high from a historical perspective, it says. In view of better industry environment, the market could raise pharma companies' 2H sales and earnings forecasts, it adds. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

2352 ET - If tensions between Thailand and Cambodia escalate, Carabao Group could face the largest impact, followed by Bumrungrad Hospital and Mega Lifesciences, CGS International analyst Kasem Prunratanamala says in a research note. Cambodia ranked 11th among Thailand's export destinations in 2024, with key exports including beverages, auto and motorcycle parts, engines and agricultural machinery--together accounting for over 30% of total exports. While there have been no significant effects on Thai companies so far, an escalation could hurt Carabao's 2025 earnings, as 28% of its revenue comes from energy drink sales in Cambodia, the analyst says. Bumrungrad and Mega Lifesciences could also be affected, with an estimated 6% of their 2025 revenues tied to Cambodia. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

First Capital 2Q Net Interest Income $10.4M >FCAP
Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Financial Services. Exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1055 ET - Phillips 66 reports a strong growth trajectory for its midstream business, which activist investor Elliott Investment Management has said should either be sold or spun off. Elliott, which got two of its nominees named as directors in May after a battle with the board, has said the midstream unit could be worth more than $40 billion. The segment generated $972 million in adjusted Ebitda for 2Q, up $87 million from last quarter, following the acquisition of EPIC NGL, which Phillips renamed Coastal Bend. The opening of another gas-processing plant in the Permian basin also boosted results, CEO Mark Lashier says in an interview with CNBC. "It opens up frontiers for more organic growth in our midstream business," he says. "So we really like that business, it's delivering results." (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1027 ET - The two Phillips 66 directors that activist investor Elliott Investment Management got installed after a boardroom battle with the oil refiner are "well integrated" among the other members, CEO Mark Lashier says in an interview with CNBC. Describing the fight with Elliott as "a big contest," Lashier says the Elliott nominees, Sigmund Cornelius and Michael Heim, are aligned with everyone else on expanding shareholder value and are bringing diverse perspectives as the board continuously reexamines its strategy. "Those perspectives are on the table, they're in the discussion," he says. The CEO pushes back when Cornelius and Heim are referenced as "Elliott board members." "They're Phillips 66 board members now," he says. "Their fiduciary responsibility is to all of the shareholders." (dean.seal@wsj.com)

0834 ET - EFG International's shares have had a strong run over the past few years after a string of positive results and surprises, Vontobel says in a research note after the Swiss bank's half-year print. "The strongly increased valuation seems to already anticipate further accretive M&A deals and/or higher financial targets to be announced at the upcoming Investor Day on Nov. 25," analyst Andreas Venditti writes. The bank is on track to exceed its targets from its previous three-year plan, except in terms of its cost-to-income ratio given the increased investments in hiring after Credit Suisse's collapse. But EFG has executed well and is on the lookout for more acquisitions, so it could aim higher when it lays out its next plan, he says. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0820 ET - Deutsche Bank's current trading adequately reflects the improved outlook for the German bank, ODDO BHF says in a research note. The group reported second-quarter results ahead of expectations with cost-control initiatives on track, analysts Roland Pfaender and Steven Gould write. Management expects a significant tailwind from Germany's planned infrastructure investment and defense spending initiatives, which will create business opportunities for the bank. Meanwhile, steps toward setting up a European investment and savings union are also set to be a boon for its asset management and private bank activities. Yet, this optimism is already priced into the stock and there is more upside with other names in the sector, the analysts say. The brokerage lowers its rating to neutral from outperform but lifts the target price to 29 euros from 26 euros. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0614 ET - Another policy rate cut by the European Central Bank this year shouldn't dent the earnings of the continent's banks, Keefe, Bruyette & Woods say in a research note after the central bank held rates steady at 2%. The market pencils in another cut by the end of the year though this seems far from certain given the ECB's reassuring and balanced press conference, analysts write. "As banks increase their [net interest income] resilience, each 25 basis point parallel shift is worth just 1% of sector earnings," they say. KBW continues to see the upward sloping yield curve as an important offset for bank earnings. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0510 ET - NatWest is well placed to weather a potential credit downturn given the resilience of its customers, the bank's finance chief tells CNBC. The bank's economic assumptions haven't moved materially despite all the noise, Katie Murray says. Impairment charges, which reflect bad loans and is a key metric for the group to see how financially healthy people are, are at historically low levels, she says. Signs of an economic deterioration aren't showing up in its numbers. "It's not something we see in the day to day activity of how people are behaving, the lending that people are taking out, or the money that they're depositing, or the investments we're seeing into shares," Murray adds. Shares are up 1.6% and 27% year to date. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0505 ET - The new midterm targets outlined by Sabadell at its investor day show that it is buckling up for growth, JB Capital says in a research note. This strengthens the view that suitor BBVA has to increase its offer price to secure meaningful acceptance, analysts write. They calculate that the offer can be enhanced by up to 48%. The Spanish bank's growth ambitions--driven by corporate and consumer loans as mortgages should align with the market--seem reasonable and cost of risk remains under control, they say. The lender's aim to add two to three percentage points to its 8% market share in the coming years seems realistic and in line with the country's nominal GDP growth expectations, they add. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0439 ET - Deutsche Bank's shares had a sharp rise on what was an average set of results and limited evidence of revenue progression in its corporate and private bank, Citi says in a research note. Citi cuts its rating on the German bank to sell from neutral. While reaction to the quarterly print seems to have been driven by commentary provided on regulatory risk-weighted asset inflation risk, this wasn't much of a concern for Citi analysts. Their fears are focused on whether Deutsche Bank will be able to meet its revenue targets and the higher bar for capital returns, they say. "The bank has become a 'concept stock' but after [an around] 60% re-rating, we would be very cautious on allocating more capital here, until there is clear evidence of earnings support flowing through," they say. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0411 ET - Close Brothers' disposal of its Winterflood unit for 104 million pounds in cash provides it with a capital buffer for the car loan probe ruling, Shore Capital says in a research note. "We see this as a decent price for a business that in recent times has been operating around a break-even level due to a combination of cyclical and structural factors, albeit perhaps less good if looked through the lens of historical profitability," analyst Gary Greenwood writes. Any excess capital after the probe is expected to be reinvested in other parts of the business or returned to shareholders, he says. Shares are up 11% at 455 pence. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0410 ET - NatWest's quarterly print shows that the bank is in a sweet spot after the government returned it to private ownership, Interactive Investor says in a market comment. The U.K. lender is the investor favorite in the sector and its results justify that optimism, analyst Richard Hunter writes. "It remains to be seen whether this new-found freedom will enable a more aggressive acquisition policy," he adds. The bank's significant cash generation brings up the dilemma of whether to boost shareholder returns, invest heavily in the business or pursue an acquisition, he says. The group also raised its returns and income guidance for the year and announced a 750 million pound share buyback, which has been well-received by investors. Shares rise 2% to 513 pence. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0350 ET - Close Brothers Group shares jumped as much as 12% to their highest price since September after the U.K. bank sold its execution services and securities business Winterflood to Marex Group for around 104 million pounds. "This is a positive transaction for CBG, particularly given speculation as recently as April that the business may be sold for only circa 40 million pounds," Keefe, Bruyette & Woods analysts Edward Firth and Elise Yu Ge write in a research note. The deal also simplifies the group and removes a business that brought in considerable levels of uncertainty and volatility over its earnings outlook, they add.(elena.vardon@wsj.com)

2159 ET - Singapore banks are likely to weather continuing trade-induced volatility, UOB Kay Hian's Jonathan Koh says in a note. Higher U.S. reciprocal tariffs are set to kick in Aug. 1, which could drive up near-term inflation. The Federal Reserve is therefore likely to delay interest-rate cuts, which could benefit Singapore's lenders. Fed Chair Jerome Powell's term expires next May. A more dovish Fed chair could lead to higher inflation, too. Koh keeps his overweight rating on Singapore's banking sector, as lenders provide attractive dividend yields supported by capital management. The brokerage names OCBC as its top pick, maintaining its buy call and S$19.25 target price on OCBC given the bank's focus on Asean trade and investment flows. Shares of OCBC are last at S$17.21. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

data.title: Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1514 ET - Oil futures end the session and the week lower in rangebound trade, with tariff concerns tempered by the U.S. reaching several trade deals and moving toward an agreement with the EU. "We're in a peak summer lull, I think there's just not much of an appetite for putting on significant directional risk," says Ben Hoff of Societe Generale. "The crude market has a fair bit of human psychology baked into it, and we've just come off a period of massively elevated geopolitical risk with pretty significant price swings," he says, referring to the Israel-Iran conflict. The next catalyst will likely be more anchored in fundamentals. "We're quite bearish from a build perspective for 4Q25 and into 2026, but the upside risks have also increased somewhat." WTI settles down 1.3% at $65.16 a barrel and Brent falls 1.1% to $68.44, for weekly losses of 1.3% and 1.2%, respectively. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1326 ET - The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. fell by seven this week to 415, down 67 from a year ago and the fewest in almost four years, according to oil services company Baker Hughes. "The U.S. oil-directed rig count has yet to stabilize, as the sharp decline following the OPEC+ pivot earlier this year continues, but the effect on production will likely not show up until Q4 25," Amarpreet Singh of Barclays says in a note. But while there is a lag between the onset of drilling and first production, "the EIA's weekly estimates suggest production in the U.S. could be rolling over already," he adds. Natural gas rigs, meanwhile, rose by five this week to 122, or 21 more than a year ago, Baker Hughes reports. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1055 ET - Phillips 66 reports a strong growth trajectory for its midstream business, which activist investor Elliott Investment Management has said should either be sold or spun off. Elliott, which got two of its nominees named as directors in May after a battle with the board, has said the midstream unit could be worth more than $40 billion. The segment generated $972 million in adjusted Ebitda for 2Q, up $87 million from last quarter, following the acquisition of EPIC NGL, which Phillips renamed Coastal Bend. The opening of another gas-processing plant in the Permian basin also boosted results, CEO Mark Lashier says in an interview with CNBC. "It opens up frontiers for more organic growth in our midstream business," he says. "So we really like that business, it's delivering results." (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1036 ET - Phillips 66 CEO Mark Lashier says the California administration has been "very supportive" of its upcoming closure of a refinery near Los Angeles, which is slated to cease operations in 4Q. Driving that support is the company's plan to resupply the refined products that will be going away, the CEO says. Phillips has already done that in Northern California when it converted a facility there from crude oil to renewable feedstocks, so it has "a proven ability to bring in feedstocks from outside California," Lashier says. Asked whether the business environment in California had become too costly to operate in, the CEO responds that the LA facility was very old, had challenging inefficiencies, was its highest-cost refiner and wasn't contributing meaningfully to earnings. "We were at a point where we would have to recapitalize that asset and we just didn't see the path forward for profitability," he says. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1027 ET - The two Phillips 66 directors that activist investor Elliott Investment Management got installed after a boardroom battle with the oil refiner are "well integrated" among the other members, CEO Mark Lashier says in an interview with CNBC. Describing the fight with Elliott as "a big contest," Lashier says the Elliott nominees, Sigmund Cornelius and Michael Heim, are aligned with everyone else on expanding shareholder value and are bringing diverse perspectives as the board continuously reexamines its strategy. "Those perspectives are on the table, they're in the discussion," he says. The CEO pushes back when Cornelius and Heim are referenced as "Elliott board members." "They're Phillips 66 board members now," he says. "Their fiduciary responsibility is to all of the shareholders." (dean.seal@wsj.com)

0957 ET - Oil futures are on track to end the week little changed, supported by the U.S. inching toward a trade deal with the EU and a second weekly draw in U.S. crude oil stocks, while additional OPEC+ output keeps a lid on advances. The negative reaction yesterday to the Trump administration's authorizing Chevron to resume production in Venezuela was brief. "The thought is that this could bring around 250,000 b/d of heavy crude supplies to the U.S.--as opposed to Asian buyers," Alex Hodes of StoneX says in a note. "This distillate-rich crude oil could help alleviate some tightness in the fuel." WTI is off 0.1% at $65.93 a barrel, and Brent is flat at $69.18 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0549 ET - The market is nervous about the sustainability of TotalEnergies' unfunded shareholder distributions, HSBC analysts write. The concerns, coupled with a jump in net debt, caused shares to drop 3.5% on Thursday despite the French energy major reporting only a small miss to earnings expectations, they write. Reassurance around second-half cash flows didn't quell the concerns. A softer environment in its LNG and downstream division could threaten its cash flow guidance, they write. TotalEnergies' dividend growth has been generous in recent years but it must be reined in, the analysts say. They cut their forecast for annual dividend per share growth to between 3% to 3.5% from between 5% to 6%. Shares fall 0.4% at 51.25 euros. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0443 ET - Eni delivered a good set of results, with net profit and earnings before interest and taxes beating expectations, Jefferies analysts Giacomo Romeo andKai Ye Loh write. This is mainly due to a strong performance in its gas and liquefied natural gas division and a lower general tax rate, they write. Investors will likely question Eni's management on the details of its joint venture with Petronas, sources of cost reductions and the timeline for final investment decisions on Friday's earnings call, they write. The call is scheduled for 12:00 GMT. Shares trade flat at 14.408 euros.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0417 ET - Eni is set for a strong second half of the year and 2026 as its divestment plan and self-help measures deliver results, RBC Capital Markets' Biraj Borkhataria and Adnan Dhanani write. The Italian oil major's balance sheet is more resilient than it has been for many years and free cash flow is growing, they write. Eni reported a decent set of second-quarter results, with a strong performance in its global gas and LNG unit delivering a 5% beat to expectations for group earnings before interest and taxes, they add. Shares trade flat at 14.40 euros. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

data.title: Basic Materials Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Basic Materials. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0231 ET - Market sentiment on lithium stocks has improved due to a potential fall in lithium supply, DBS analysts write in a note. Zangge Mining's production halt in its subsidy Golmud due to compliance issues, combined with expected declines in overseas lithium supply from Chile, Australia's Bald Hill mine, and Africa in 3Q, have fuelled expectations reduced lithium supply, they say. Also, Chinese government's recent campaign to curb excessive competition has improved market sentiment for industries facing over-capacity, including lithium, they say. The marginal supply reduction in 3Q could support a short-term rebound in lithium prices, they add. DBS maintains a buy rating on Tianqi Lithium shares and raises its target price to HK$50 from HK$35. They were recently at HK$40.35.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

2211 ET - Gold miner Newmont is target to meet FY guidance following stronger-than-expected 2Q production results, says Citi analyst Alexander Hacking in a note. "1H production from core mines was 2.8 million ounces, leaving the company well on track for [its] 5.6-million-ounce guidance," given output from Nevada Gold Mines is 2H weighted, says Hacking. He also highlights Newmont's record free cash flow, boosted by strong gold prices. Citi has a buy rating on Newmont, and expects the stock to outperform, says Hacking. Newmont is up 3.3% in Sydney at A$94.92. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2052 ET - Whitehaven's 4Q run-of-mine output beats consensus expectations and translates to strong sales, Jefferies analyst Daniel Roden says in a note. The miner's realized price is 5% lower than analysts anticipated, but offset by a strong result on costs, says Roden. Net debt is also better than expected. Roden says Whitehaven's balance sheet looks resilient at a time when coal prices have been weak. "Good operational control and an improving coal market outlook enhance expectations of deleveraging and capital returns," he says. Jefferies has a buy rating and A$6.40 target on Whitehaven. The stock is down 0.1% at A$7.02, outperforming Australia's materials index, which is sharply lower.(rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2000 ET - Whitehaven recorded a good cost performance for FY25, says Citi analyst Paul McTaggart, after what he calls a "robust" 4Q result from the Australian coal miner. McTaggart says Whitehaven's 4Q saleable production was 9% higher than Citi expected. Cost guidance for FY26 will come next month with Whitehaven's FY fiscal results, he notes. Citi has a buy rating on the stock, with a target price of A$7.00. Whitehaven ended Thursday at A$7.03/share. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.title: Auto & Transport Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Auto and Transport sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0622 ET - Hesai Group is likely to benefit from the rapid expansion of China's robotaxi fleet, leveraging its solid position in the robotaxi LiDAR market, Daiwa analysts write in a note. Hesai may maintain robust LiDAR delivery momentum and reach breakeven in 2Q, they add. The company has long dominated the global robotaxi LiDAR market thanks to the performance and cost advantages of its mechanical LiDAR. Chinese robotaxi operators began large-scale commercialization of their services in 2025, cutting costs by adopting semi-solid-state LiDAR to replace more expensive mechanical versions, an area where Hesai again holds a competitive edge, they say. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0617 ET - The more-than $600 million tariff hit to Hyundai Motor's 2Q operating profit was double what HSBC expected, but analyst Will Cho thinks the tariff narrative has peaked. HSBC's sensitivity analysis suggests that a tariff reduction to 15% from 25% for South Korea could boost its 2025 operating profit forecast for the automaker by 13%. Mitigation efforts such as reducing incentives, increasing local production and diversifying supply chains will likely further blunt the tariff impact, Cho says. He also views the company's record revenue, which came despite a weak dollar, as encouraging. HSBC maintains a buy rating on the carmaker with a target price of KRW270,000, saying the stock offers compelling value following a sharp pullback. Shares last closed at KRW216,500. (farah.elias@wsj.com)

0614 ET - Traton's downgrade to its 2025 guidance suggests double-digit cuts to consensus estimates for next year are inevitable, Bernstein analysts say in a research note. "[This year's] headwinds are well known and we would be happy--as many investors clearly have been given the strength of truck share prices--to look through this if 2026 expectations looked reasonable," the analysts say. However, the German truck and bus maker's new guidance implies it will need earnings to grow by a third in 2026 to meet consensus estimates, according to Bernstein. Shares fall 2.7%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0558 ET - Traton cut its full-year guidance after a first-half performance that was in line with expectations, and its new outlook raises questions about pricing and order momentum, Bernstein analysts say in a research note. First-half orders were slightly lower than expected, the analysts say. While a reduction in the German bus and truck maker's guidance for full-year revenue and unit sales expectations was fully anticipated, its new profitability guidance is below consensus estimates, according to Bernstein. This implies a decline in Traton's adjusted operating margin for the second half similar to that of the first and double-digit downgrades to consensus estimates, Bernstein says. Shares fall 2.9%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0546 ET - Pony AI is likely to break even on a unit level for its robotaxi fleet starting in 2026, Daiwa analysts write in a note. As the company's fleet grows, it may report positive operating profit and net profit from 2029, the analysts add. Pony AI could see revenue growth accelerate from 2027 when its robotaxi fleet is projected to reach 10,000 across China's four biggest cities. Pony AI is at the forefront of shaping the future of urban mobility, and has developed an innovative business model for ride-hailing and is actively engaging with regulators to enable the commercialization of autonomous driving, Daiwa says. It initiates coverage of the stock with a buy rating at a target price of $20.00. Shares last closed at $14.45. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0500 ET - Volkswagen's new guidance is already under pressure due to a profit warning from subsidiary Traton, especially at the free cash flow level, UBS analyst Patrick Hummel writes. The bank expects slight negative consensus revisions, mainly on free cash flow. Volkswagen now sees flat revenue on year from up to 5% growth previously, with an operating profit margin of 4%-5%. Free cash flow is now guided at 1 billion-3 billion euros. "However, the new guidance doesn't include last night's profit warning from Traton, which shaves off 1 billion euros free cash flow and around 500 million euros of operating profit at VW group level." Shares rise 2.6%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0438 ET - Grab could gain market share in the delivery space through its new budget-friendly products, says DBS's Sachin Mittal in a note. The group is expected to see higher order frequency, as such products include lower-cost delivery options and are likely to expand the company's Southeast Asia customer base. Its current monthly active users in the delivery and ride-hailing businesses cover only about 6% of the population in the region. DBS raises its FY 2025 and FY 2026 forecasts for adjusted Ebitda by 1% and 4%, respectively, and maintains a buy call on the stock. It raises its target price to $6.35 from $5.68. Shares last traded at US$5.34. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

0341 ET - Volkswagen earnings show that tariffs are biting as the company lowered its full-year guidance, Bernstein analysts write. Second-quarter results are mildly ahead of consensus, with VW noting an encouraging underlying performance at VW Passenger Cars excluding tariff impacts and restructuring charges. The operating result was 3.0% above Visible Alpha consensus of 3.7 billion euros, Bernstein adds. Audi and Porsche were the biggest underperformers for the quarter, with Audi's operating profit down 64% on year and Porsche's down 91%. The pair's operating profits missed Visible Alpha consensus by 48% and 61%, respectively, the bank says. Shares fall 3.3%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0311 ET - Volkswagen's core brands of VW, Skoda, Seat, Cupra and Volkswagen Commercial Vehicles showed resilience in the second quarter, while the group guidance cut was mostly as expected, Jefferies analysts write. The company posted a resilient second-quarter margin of 4.3% after a 1.2 billion-euro tariff impact in the quarter, they say. The core brands delivered a 6.3% margin from 3.8% last year, while there was the expected poor showings from Audi and Porsche. Audi is due to improve from the third quarter, but Porsche's guidance still looks at risk, the bank adds. As expected, VW reduced guidance to an operating profit margin of 4%-5% and compares with consensus at 4.7%. The working capital outflow is still a weak link, they add. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0251 ET - Ningbo Tuopu Group's revenue growth is likely to weaken in 2Q on lower-than-expected sales at key customers including Tesla, Seres and BYD, Jefferies analysts write in a note. The auto-parts supplier's management expects single-digit percentage revenue growth in 2Q, they say. The relatively low content value per vehicle for Geely limited contribution to Tuopu's revenue despite strong volume growth, Jefferies says. Ningbo Tuopu also supplies components for Tesla's robotaxi, with a content value per vehicle of around CNY2,000. The company says that its D&A expenses account for 5% to 7% of revenue, and it expects the figure to approach or exceed 7% when revenue growth slows, they add. Jefferies maintains its buy rating for the stock. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0229 ET - SIA Engineering's earnings outlook seems positive, but appears reflected in the share price, CGS International's Raymond Yap says in a research report. Benefits from the company's new contracts with Singapore Airlines group are expected to become more evident in its earnings in future quarters. However, start-up costs and gestation losses for the company's new facilities are likely to increase in the coming quarters as progress is made toward full commercial operations. The brokerage maintains the stock's target price of S$3.10, but downgrades the rating to reduce from add as it reckons the share price adequately reflects the positive earnings trajectory. Shares are up 0.3% at S$3.31. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0201 ET - ComfortDelGro's "low-beta" stock status could change soon, Maybank Research's Eric Ong reckons. Its stock is supposedly one of the prime candidates to benefit from the MAS' deployment of its S$5 billion Equity Market Development Programme, the analyst says in a research report. Also, autonomous vehicles may be on the way soon. The multimodal transport operator launched its first two-year robotaxi pilot program in China's Guangzhou in partnership with Pony.ai, an autonomous mobility company, the analyst notes. The brokerage maintains the stock's buy rating, but raises the target price to S$1.70 from S$1.64 to reflect lower weighted average cost of capital. Shares are 5.8% higher at S$1.63. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Bitcoin derivatives data questions the strength of BTC’s $115K support
data.description:
Key takeaways:

  • Bitcoin options and futures data suggest traders are neutral despite a 7% drop from the peak.

  • Stablecoin demand in China remains steady, showing marginal fear in crypto markets.

Bitcoin (BTC) dropped 4% between Thursday and Friday, falling below $115,000 for the first time in two weeks. The correction coincided with the monthly derivatives expiry, which wiped out $390 million worth of futures contracts, equivalent to 14% of open interest. 

To determine if this event altered traders’ longer-term expectations, it’s important to examine Bitcoin futures and options indicators.

Under normal conditions, monthly Bitcoin futures trade at a 5% to 10% annualized premium over spot markets to compensate for the longer settlement period. The current 7% premium falls within that neutral range and is close to Monday’s 8% level. At first glance, the data suggests no shift in investor sentiment, despite Bitcoin’s $4,700 price drop.

Bitcoin reached a record high of $123,181 on July 14, but the last time futures data signaled bullish momentum was in early February. That timing aligns with the United States imposing import tariffs and the disappointment over the US Federal Reserve maintaining interest rates, despite January’s relatively calm Consumer Price Index (CPI) reading of 3% year-over-year.

To validate whether the neutral stance in Bitcoin futures accurately reflects investor sentiment, one should assess the BTC options skew. When traders anticipate a correction, put (sell) options tend to command a premium over call (buy) options, driving the 25% delta skew above 6%.

On Friday, Bitcoin’s 25% delta skew surged to 10%, a rare stress level last seen nearly four months ago. However, the elevated fear was short-lived, as the skew quickly returned to a balanced 1% level. This signals that whales and market makers are pricing similar risks for both upward and downward price moves.

Bitcoin traders cautiously observe 80K BTC wallet transfers 

Bitcoin derivatives suggest that traders are not particularly eager to buy near $116,000, but they also have not panicked after the 7% drop from the all-time high. That is somewhat reassuring given the concerns surrounding the entity that unloaded a portion of its 80,000 BTC balance at Galaxy Digital, according to Nansen CEO Alex Svanevik.

Stablecoin demand in China provides additional insight. Strong retail activity typically drives stablecoins to trade at a 2% or higher premium to the official US dollar rate. Conversely, a discount greater than 0.5% often signals market fear, as traders exit crypto positions.

Currently, Tether (USDT) is trading at a modest 0.5% discount in China. This indicates that Bitcoin’s recent price dip has not significantly affected cryptocurrency demand in the region. Even with Bitcoin reaching a new all-time high, stablecoin inflows and outflows have remained largely unchanged over the past two weeks.

Overall, Bitcoin traders seem more concerned about the potential escalation of global trade tensions or a US economic recession, both of which could trigger broader risk aversion and weigh on Bitcoin. Still, the current lack of enthusiasm in Bitcoin derivatives does not reflect any critical issues within the crypto markets, which is constructive for the $115,000 resistance level.

This article is for general information purposes and is not intended to be and should not be taken as legal or investment advice. The views, thoughts, and opinions expressed here are the author’s alone and do not necessarily reflect or represent the views and opinions of Cointelegraph.

data.title: GRUPO SIMEC ANNOUNCES RESULTS OF OPERATIONS FOR THE FIRST SIX MONTHS OF 2025
data.description:
GUADALAJARA, Mexico, July 25, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Grupo Simec, S.A.B. de C.V. (NYSE: SIM) ("Simec") announced today its results of operations for the sixth-month period ended June 30th, 2025.

Comparative first six months of 2025 vs. first six months of 2024

Net Sales

Net sales decreased 9% by the combination of less shipments of finished steel products (11%) and a higher average sales price of 3% compared to the same period of 2024, the sales decreased from Ps. 16,279 million in the first half of 2024 to Ps. 14,835 million in the first half of 2025. Shipments of finished steel products decreased 11% to 901 thousand tons in the first half of 2025 compared to 1 million 015 thousand tons in the first half of 2024. Total sales outside of Mexico on the first half of 2025 decreased 10% to Ps. 6,573 million compared with Ps. 7,287 million in the first half of 2024. Total sales in Mexico decreased 8% from Ps. 8,992 million in the first half of 2024 to Ps. 8,262 million in the first half of 2025.

Cost of Sales

Cost of sales decreased 9% from Ps. 12,232 million in the first half of 2024, to Ps. 11,167 million in the first half of 2025. Cost of sales as a percentage of net sales represented 75% for both periods 2025 and 2024. The average cost of finished steel produced in the first half of 2025 compared to the same period of 2024 increased 3% mainly due to higher scrap cost.

Gross Profit

Gross profit of the Company in the first half of 2025 was of Ps. 3,668 million compared to Ps. 4,047 million in the first half of 2024. Gross profit as percentage of net sales represented 25% for both periods 2025 and 2024.

Selling, General and Administrative Expenses

Selling, general and administrative expenses increased 11%, from Ps. 1,176 million in the first half of 2024 to Ps. 1,307 million in the same period 2025, selling, general and administrative expenses as a percentage of net sales represented 7% of the net sales in the first half of 2024 and 9% in the first half of 2025.

Other Income (Expenses,) net

The Company recorded other income net for Ps. 263 million in the first half of 2025 million compared to other income net Ps. 45 million in the same period of 2024.

Operating Profit

Operating profit decreased 10% from Ps. 2,916 million for the first half of 2024 compared to Ps. 2,624 million in the first half of 2025. Operating income as percentage of net sales was 18% in the first half of 2025 and 2024. The decrease in operating profit is mainly due to less shipments of finished steel products.

Ebitda

The Ebitda of the Company decreased 7% from Ps. 3,413 million in the first half of 2024 (result of net income of Ps. 5,435 million, less minority stake of Ps. 1 million, plus income taxes of Ps.291 million, less comprehensive financial income of Ps. 2,809 million, plus depreciation of Ps. 497 million), compared to Ebitda of Ps 3,165 million in the first semester of 2025 (result of net income of Ps. 304 million, less minority stake of Ps. 1 million, plus income taxes of Ps. 476 million, plus comprehensive financial cost of Ps. 1,845 million, plus depreciation of Ps. 541 million).

Likewise, the Company recorded a net interest income of Ps. 487 million for the first half of 2025 compared with a net interest income of Ps. 737 million in 2024, in the first half of 2024 there is a record of net other financial income for Ps. 42 million.

Income Taxes

The Company recorded a net tax expense of Ps. 476 million during the first half of 2025, (comprised for a current tax expense of Ps. 383 million and expense for deferred tax of Ps. 93 million) compared with net tax expense of Ps. 291 million to the first half of 2024 (comprised for a current tax expense of Ps. 412 million and income for deferred tax of Ps. 121 million).

As a result of the foregoing, the Company recorded a decrease in net income of 94% from of Ps. 5,435 million in the first half of 2024 to Ps. 304 million in the same period of 2025.

Comparative second quarter of 2025 vs. first quarter of 2025

Net Sales

Net sales of the Company decreased 9% in the second quarter of 2025 compared to the first quarter of the same period, from of Ps. 7,783 million during the first quarter of 2025 to Ps. 7,052 million in the second quarter of 2025. Shipments of finished steel products decreased from 476 thousand tons in the first quarter of 2025 to 425 thousand tons in the second quarter of the same year. Total sales outside of Mexico in the second quarter of 2025 decreased 11% to Ps. 3,104 million compared to Ps. 3,469 million of the first quarter of the same year. Domestic sales decreased from Ps. 4,314 million in the first quarter of 2025 to Ps. 3,948 million in the second quarter of the same year. The sales decreased mainly because of lower shipments of finished steel products (11%) and a higher average sales price of 1%.

Cost of Sales

Cost of sales decreased 7% from Ps. 5,786 million in the first quarter of 2025 to Ps. 5,381million in the second quarter of 2025. Cost of sales as a percentage of net sales represented 74% for the first quarter of 2025 compared to 76% in the second quarter of the same year, the average cost of sales per ton had an increase of 4%.

Gross Profit

Gross profit of the Company for the second quarter of 2025 decreased 16% from of Ps. 1,997 million in the first quarter of 2025 to Ps. 1,671 million in the second quarter of the same year. Gross profit as a percentage of net sales represented 26% for the first quarter of 2025 compared to 24% in the second quarter of the same year.

Selling, General and Administrative Expense

Selling, general and administrative expense increased 6%, from Ps. 633 million in the first quarter of 2025 to Ps. 674 million in the second quarter of the same year, and as percentage of net sales represented 8% in the first quarter of 2025 compared to 10% in the second quarter of the same year.

Other (Expenses) Income, net

The Company recorded other income net for Ps. 201 million during the second quarter of 2025 compared to other income net for Ps. 62 million in the first quarter of 2025.

Operating Income

Operating income decreased 16%, from of Ps. 1,426 million in the first quarter of 2025 compared to Ps. 1,198 million in the second quarter of the same year. Operating income as percentage of net sales represented 18% for the first quarter of 2025 compared to 17% in the second quarter of the same year.

Ebitda

The Ebitda was Ps. 1,692 million in the first quarter of 2025 (result of net income of Ps. 1,305 million, less minority stake of Ps. 1 million, plus income taxes of Ps. 179 million, less comprehensive financial income of Ps. 57 million, plus depreciation of Ps. 266 million), compared to Ebitda of Ps 1,473 million in the second quarter of 2025 (result of net loss of Ps. 1,000 million, less minority stake of Ps. 1 million, plus income taxes of Ps. 297 million, plus comprehensive financial cost of Ps. 1,902 million, plus depreciation of Ps. 275 million).

Liquidity and Capital Resources

As of June 30th, 2025, Simec's total consolidated debt consisted of U.S. $302,000 of 8 7/8% medium-term notes ("MTN's") due 1998, or Ps. 5.7 million (accrued interest on June 30th, 2025 was U.S. $856,000, or Ps. 16.1 million). As June 30, 2024, Simec's total consolidated debt consisted of U.S. $302,000 of 8 7/8% medium-term notes ("MTN's") due 1998, or Ps. 5.5 million (accrued interest on June 30, 2024 was U.S. $812,000, or Ps. 14.9 million).

Comparative second quarter of 2025 vs. second quarter of 2024

Net Sales

Net sales of the Company decreased 16% from Ps. 8,394 million during the second quarter of 2024 to Ps. 7,052 million in the second quarter of 2025. Sales in tons of finished steel products decreased 21% from 536 thousand tons in the second quarter of 2024 compared with 425 thousand tons in the second quarter of 2025. Sales outside of Mexico in the second quarter of 2025 decreased 14% from Ps. 3,593 million in the second quarter of 2024 to Ps. 3,104 million in the second quarter of 2025. Domestic sales decreased 18% from Ps. 4,801 million in the second quarter of 2024 to Ps. 3,948 million in the second quarter of 2025. The decrease in sales in the second quarter of 2025 compared to the second quarter of 2024 is due to an increase in the average sales price of 6% and a decrease in the volume of shipments approximately of 111 thousand of tons that represents a 21% decrease.

Cost of Sales

Cost of sales decreased 15% in the second quarter of 2025 compared to the second quarter of 2024 from Ps. 6,356 million in the second quarter of 2024 to Ps. 5,381 million in the second quarter of 2025. With respect to sales, the cost of sales of the second quarter of 2025 and 2024 represented 76%. The average cost of sales by ton of steel products increased 7% in the second quarter of 2025 versus the second quarter of 2024, due to the increase in costs of certain raw materials mainly scrap.

Gross (Loss) Profit

Gross profit of the Company for the second quarter of 2025 was to Ps. 1,671 million compared to Ps. 2,038 million in the second quarter of 2024, this represented a decrease of 18% between both periods. Gross profit as a percentage of net sales for the second quarter of 2025 and 2024 was 24%. The decrease in gross profit is mainly due to a lower volume of finished steel products shipped, in the second quarter of 2025 compared to the second quarter of 2024.

Selling, General and Administrative Expense

The selling, general and administrative expenses increased 16% in the second quarter of 2025 from Ps. 580 million in the second quarter of 2024 to Ps. 674 million in the second quarter of 2025. Selling, general and administrative expense as a percentage of net sales represented 10% in the second quarter of 2025 compared to 7% of the second quarter of 2024.

Other Income (Expenses), net

The company recorded other income net of Ps. 201 million in the second quarter of 2025 compared with other income net of Ps. 40 million for the second quarter of 2024.

Operating (Loss) Income

Operating income was to Ps. 1,198 million in the second quarter 2025 compared to Ps. 1,498 million in the second quarter of 2024. The operating income as a percentage of net sales was 17% in the second quarter of 2025, compared to 18% in the second quarter of 2024. The decrease in operating profit was due to a lower volume of finished steel products shipped.

Ebitda

The Ebitda was Ps. 1,747 million in the second quarter of 2024 (result of net income of Ps. 3,979 million, plus income taxes of Ps. 181 million, less comprehensive financial income of Ps. 2,662 million, plus depreciation of Ps. 249 million), compared to Ebitda of Ps 1,473 million in the second quarter of 2025 (result of net loss of Ps. 1,000 million, less minority stake of Ps. 1 million, plus income taxes of Ps. 297 million, plus comprehensive financial cost of Ps. 1,902 million, plus depreciation of Ps. 275 million).

data.title: Dell, Bitfarms, Bloom Energy, and More Stocks See Action From Activist Investors — Barrons.com
data.description:
These disclosures are from 13Ds filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission. 13Ds are filed within 10 days of an entity's attaining more than 5% in any class of a company's securities. Subsequent changes in holdings or intentions must be reported in amended filings. This material is from July 17 through July 23, 2025. Source: VerityData

Original Filings

Genco Shipping & Trading (GNK) Diana Shipping disclosed a new position in the ocean shipping company of 3,315,902 shares. Diana Shipping purchased 2,939,721 of those shares from May 21 through July 17, 2025, at per share prices ranging from $12.89 to $15.05. Following those purchases, Diana Shipping owns 7.7% of Genco Shipping & Trading's outstanding stock. Shares of Genco Shipping have gained roughly 10.4% in value since the beginning of this year.

Decreases in Holdings

Dell Technologies (DELL) Silver Lake lowered its stake in the computer maker to 58,887,472 shares. Silver Lake and its affiliates did so through the sale of 2,707,804 Dell Technologies shares from June 9 through July 16, 2025, at per share prices ranging from $112.83 to $128.27. Following the sales, Silver Lake owns 14.7% of Dell Technologies' outstanding stock. Shares of Dell Technologies have gained approximately 9.1% in value since the beginning of this year.

Bloom Energy (BE) SK ecoplant reduced its stake in the fuel cell provider to 13,491,701 shares. SK ecoplant did so through the sale of 10 million Bloom Energy shares on July 10, 2025, in a Rule 144 block sale at a price of $27.60 per share. Following the latest sale, SK ecoplant continues to own 5.8% of Bloom Energy's outstanding stock. Shares of Bloom Energy have gained about 14.5% in value in 2025.

LENZ Therapeutics (LENZ) Versant Venture Capital VI lowered its stake in the late-stage biopharmaceutical company to 1,575,899 shares. Versant Venture Capital did so through the sale of 391,376 LENZ Therapeutics shares from June 27 through July 10, 2025, at per share prices ranging from $30.47 to $34. Following the latest sales, Versant Venture Capital continues to own 5.6% of LENZ Therapeutics' outstanding stock. Shares of LENZ Therapeutics have gained roughly 16.8% in value since the beginning of this year.

Bitfarms (BITF) Riot Platforms reduced its stake in the Bitcoin-mining company to 54,745,063 shares. Riot Platforms did so through the sale of 7,230,637 Bitfarms shares from July 9 through July 16, 2025, at per share prices ranging from $1.04 to $1.15. Following those sales, Riot Platforms continues to own 9.8% of Bitfarms' outstanding stock. Shares of Bitfarms have lost roughly 21.1% in value since the beginning of this year.

ARS Pharmaceuticals (SPRY) Deerfield Capital lowered its stake in the pharmaceutical company to 8,633,537 shares. Deerfield Capital did so through the sale of 1,090,971 ARS Pharmaceuticals shares from July 10 through July 21, 2025, at per share prices ranging from $18 to $18.54. Following the latest sales, Deerfield Capital continues to own 8.8% of ARS Pharmaceuticals' outstanding stock. Shares of ARS Pharmaceuticals have gained approximately 68.1% in value since the beginning of this year.

data.title: The Daily: Galaxy moves $3.5 billion in bitcoin, ex-BlackRock exec joins Ethereum treasury firm, OSL raises $300 million, and more
data.description:
The following article is adapted from The Block’s newsletter, The Daily, which comes out on weekday afternoons.

Happy Friday! Here’s what you need to know in crypto today.

Galaxy Digital moved nearly 30,000 BTC — worth about $3.5 billion — to exchanges on Friday, part of a broader transfer tied to an 80,201 BTC trove from a dormant 2011-era whale. While some of the bitcoin has likely been sold, Galaxy still holds 18,504 BTC worth $2.14 billion.

Bitcoin is down over 2% in the past 24 hours, though spot BTC ETFs saw $226 million in inflows on Thursday, snapping a three-day outflow streak.

Meanwhile, crypto treasuries continue to expand. The Smarter Web Company added $26 million in BTC, Windtree secured a $500 million equity line to build a BNB treasury, and Ethereum treasury firm SharpLink appointed a former BlackRock exec as co-CEO. Hong Kong’s OSL Group also raised $300 million for global growth.

Let’s dive in.

Galaxy Digital moves $3.5B in BTC tied to dormant whale stash

  • Galaxy Digital moved nearly 30,000 BTC — worth around $3.5 billion — to exchanges on Friday, continuing a multi-day transfer of an 80,201 BTC trove linked to a dormant whale wallet from 2011, according to Arkham.
  • The transfers included a 22,610 BTC chunk early Friday, followed by several smaller deposits to venues like Binance and OKX. Galaxy still holds 18,504 BTC — worth about $2.14 billion — from the stash, per Lookonchain.
  • The Block previously reported that Galaxy first received 40,010 BTC from the whale on July 15, followed by another 40,191 BTC on July 18. So far, roughly 61,697 BTC from the whale’s cache has been deposited to exchanges, likely for sale.
  • Galaxy CEO Mike Novogratz recently said bitcoin could hit $150,000 this year — though he expects ether to outperform in the short term as public firms ramp up ETH treasury purchases.

Windtree lines up $520M to build BNB treasury

  • Nasdaq-listed Windtree Therapeutics has secured up to $520 million in equity financing to build one of the largest BNB treasuries among public companies.
  • The raise includes a $500 million equity line of credit from an unnamed institutional investor and a separate $20 million placement led by Build and Build Corp. The company says 90% of the proceeds will go toward purchasing BNB, pending shareholder approval to increase its authorized shares.
  • Windtree also signed a term sheet with Kraken for custody, OTC execution, and trading support as it accelerates its pivot into crypto.
  • If fully drawn, Windtree would have access to $720 million across its recent deals. That puts it in line with other emerging non-bitcoin treasury firms — including DeFi Development Corp, BitMine Immersion, and Nano Labs — that are raising large sums to add altcoins like Solana, Ethereum, and BNB to their balance sheets.

Former BlackRock exec joins SharpLink as co-CEO

  • Ethereum treasury firm SharpLink Gaming has named former BlackRock managing director Joseph Chalom as its new co-CEO. Chalom previously led BlackRock’s digital asset partnerships, including collaborations with Anchorage, BNY Mellon, Circle, and Coinbase.
  • Chalom said he was proud of helping BlackRock launch crypto ETF products like IBIT and ETHA, and of advancing tokenization through BlackRock’s BUIDL fund.
  • “Few executives in the world have had the kind of impact Joseph has had in unlocking institutional adoption of digital assets, having pioneered BlackRock’s strategic entry into the space,” said Joseph Lubin, Ethereum co-founder and chairman of SharpLink.
  • SharpLink holds $1.3 billion in ETH and was recently overtaken by BitMine as the largest ETH treasury company. BlackRock’s Ethereum ETF crossed $10 billion in AUM this week.

Hong Kong’s OSL raises $300M for global crypto expansion

  • Hong Kong-based crypto trading platform operator OSL Group has raised $300 million in equity financing to accelerate global expansion and advance crypto payment infrastructure.
  • OSL said it will use the funds for strategic acquisitions and to build out fiat, stablecoin, and crypto-integrated payment solutions. CFO Ivan Wong called the raise a “major milestone” for the firm’s digital asset strategy.
  • The funding comes ahead of Hong Kong’s new stablecoin licensing regime, set to take effect Aug. 1. OSL has already made expansion moves, including acquiring Japanese exchange CoinBest and agreeing to purchase Indonesia’s Evergreen Crest.

The Smarter Web Company adds $26M in BTC

  • The Smarter Web Company has added 225 BTC — worth $26.4 million — to its treasury, bringing total holdings to 1,825 BTC at an average price of $109,088.
  • The UK-based web design firm, now focused on a bitcoin treasury strategy, ranks 26th globally among public bitcoin holders, per Bitcointreasuries.net.
  • The company also reported a $968K loss for the six months ended April 30. CEO Andrew Webley said bitcoin remains their preferred treasury asset for future business needs.

In the next 7 days

  • U.S. GDP and the ADP employment report are out on Wednesday, along with the Fed’s interest rate decision and Powell’s press conference.
  • Core PCE inflation data, personal income and spending, and initial jobless claims are released on Thursday.
  • The U.S. July jobs report, including payrolls and unemployment rate, is due Friday — alongside ISM manufacturing and consumer sentiment data.
  • Notable token unlocks, including Jupiter (JUP), Optimism (OP), and Sui (SUI), are set to hit the market next week.

Never miss a beat with The Block's daily digest of the most influential events happening across the digital asset ecosystem.

Foresight Ventures invests in other companies in the crypto space. Crypto exchange Bitget is an anchor LP for Foresight Ventures. data.title: CME cattle futures climb as US herd remains small
data.description:
Chicago Mercantile Exchange cattle futures turned higher on Friday, as traders anticipated U.S. government reports would show tight cattle supplies.

After the market closed, the U.S. Department of Agriculture reported that the nation's cattle herd was 94.2 million head as of July 1, a record low for that date.

In a separate report, USDA said that as of July 1, there were 2% fewer cattle in U.S. feedlots compared to a year earlier. Analysts had expected USDA to show 0.8% fewer cattle, according to a Reuters News poll.

Still, much of the market's attention early in the trading session was focused on U.S. President Donald Trump's social media posts, where he said the United States would sell "so much" beef to Australia after Canberra said it would relax import restrictions.

Almost immediately, economists, market analysts, and commodity traders interviewed by Reuters questioned Trump's assertion.

U.S. beef prices set records this year after ranchers slashed their herds due to drought that burned up pasturelands used for grazing.

Now, a ban on cattle imports from Mexico because of New World screwworm, a devastating livestock pest, and steep tariffs on Brazilian beef that are set to take effect on August 1 could further tighten U.S. meat supplies and require additional imports of Australian beef.

"Are we going to be selling a bunch of beef to Australia right now? Absolutely not," said Dan Norcini, an independent livestock trader. "The cattle numbers just aren't there."

CME August live cattle futures (LCQ25) settled up 0.675 cent at 226.475 cents per pound, while most-active October live cattle futures (LCV25) ended up 1 cent at 223.150 cents per pound.

CME August feeder cattle futures (FCQ25) ended 2.475 cents higher at 331.375 cents per pound. CME September (FCU25), October (FCV25) and November (FCX25) feeder cattle futures set fresh contract highs during the session.

Meanwhile, lean hog futures were mostly lower, as traders are growing concerned that top-buyer Mexico will roll out retaliatory tariffs on U.S. pork next month, market analysts said.

CME August lean hog futures (LHQ25) ended up 0.500 cent at 108.700 cents per pound, while most-active October (LHV25) ended 0.500 cent lower at 90.600 cents per pound.

data.title: CytoDyn Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
CytoDyn Inc., a clinical-stage biotechnology company specializing in the development of leronlimab, a monoclonal antibody targeting the CCR5 receptor, has released its annual 10-K report. The report outlines significant financial improvements, strategic initiatives, and ongoing challenges as the company continues to focus on solid-tumor oncology.

Financial Highlights

  • Operating gain (loss): $9.661 million. The company reported an operating gain compared to a loss in the previous year, primarily due to a significant reduction in research and development expenses.
  • Net income (loss): $3.745 million. The net income for the fiscal year ended May 31, 2025, was primarily due to the recovery of approximately $25.0 million in clinical expenses from the settlement of litigation, which is a non-recurring event.
  • Income (loss) per share - Basic: $0.00. The basic income per share improved from a loss in the previous year, reflecting the net income achieved.
  • Income (loss) per share - Diluted: $0.00. The diluted income per share also improved from a loss in the previous year, consistent with the basic income per share.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: CytoDyn Inc. is focused on developing leronlimab for potential use in solid-tumor oncology.
  • Current Business Strategy: The company aims to continue the Phase II trial of leronlimab in relapsed/refractory micro-satellite stable colorectal cancer, explore its potential in other solid-tumor oncology indications like metastatic triple-negative breast cancer, and develop a long-acting version of leronlimab.
  • Recent Corporate Developments: The company appointed Robert E. Hoffman as Chief Financial Officer and resolved a dispute with Amarex Clinical Research LLC favorably.
  • Leronlimab and Colorectal Cancer: The company initiated a Phase II trial for leronlimab in combination with TAS-102 and bevacizumab for metastatic colorectal cancer, with nine clinical sites approved and two patients dosed as of July 2025.
  • Leronlimab and Metastatic Triple-Negative Breast Cancer: Encouraging survival outcomes were observed in patients treated with leronlimab, with plans to resume clinical development and confirm retrospective observations.
  • Pre-Clinical Development of Long-Acting CCR5 Antagonist: CytoDyn is collaborating with a third-party company using AI tools to develop longer-acting molecules, aiming for less frequent injections and increased patient acceptance.
  • Patents and Intellectual Property: The company actively pursues patent protection and data exclusivity for leronlimab, with patents related to cancer indications potentially expiring in 2040 and beyond.
  • Regulatory Compliance: CytoDyn is subject to extensive regulation by the FDA and other authorities, with ongoing efforts to comply with requirements for product development, approval, and marketing.
  • Manufacturing Strategy: The company relies on third-party manufacturing organizations for leronlimab production and continues to explore alternative partners to support cost-efficient CMC activities.
  • Future Outlook: CytoDyn plans to focus on solid-tumor oncology, expand strategic partnerships, and secure additional funding to support its clinical development and commercialization efforts.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company is focused on the clinical development and potential commercialization of leronlimab, particularly in solid-tumor oncology. Strategic initiatives include continuing the Phase II trial of leronlimab in colorectal cancer, exploring its potential in other oncology indications, and developing a long-acting version of leronlimab. The company has entered into strategic partnerships with academic institutions and private foundations to further develop leronlimab cost-effectively.
  • Capital Management: The company engaged in several capital management activities, including closing multiple financing transactions to support business operations. It issued convertible notes with a principal amount of $28.5 million each, accruing interest at 6% per annum, maturing in April 2026. The company also conducted private placements of common stock and warrants, raising approximately $10.9 million in net cash from financing activities. Additionally, the company has 1,750 million authorized shares of common stock, with 1,249.2 million shares outstanding as of May 31, 2025.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates needing significant additional funding to execute its business strategy fully, including conducting further pre-clinical and clinical trials. It plans to pursue non-dilutive financing opportunities, such as license agreements and strategic partnerships. The company also intends to continue its debt reduction strategy and explore new capital investment opportunities to support its growth initiatives.

Challenges and Risks

  • Financial Risks: The company has low cash reserves and does not expect substantial revenues in the foreseeable future, necessitating significant additional financing, which is difficult to secure due to the low trading price of its common stock. The auditors have issued a going concern opinion, indicating substantial doubt about the company's ability to continue operations without adequate financing.
  • Operational Risks: The recruitment and retention of skilled personnel are challenging and expensive, potentially affecting drug development and commercialization activities. The company also relies heavily on third parties for clinical trials and manufacturing, which poses risks if these parties fail to meet regulatory requirements.
  • Regulatory Risks: The company is subject to oversight by the SEC, FDA, and other regulatory agencies. Investigations and proceedings by these agencies could divert management's focus and adversely affect the company's reputation and financial condition. Additionally, disruptions at the FDA and other government agencies could delay product development and approval processes.
  • Legal Risks: Ongoing litigation, including class-action lawsuits and regulatory investigations, could negatively impact the company's business and financial condition. Insurance coverage may not be sufficient to cover all related costs and damages.
  • Market Risks: The company's common stock is classified as "penny stock," which may restrict trading and limit marketability. The trading price has been volatile, and the company expects continued dilution from future financings, which could adversely affect the stock price.
  • Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the need for substantial additional funding to continue operations and pursue the development of leronlimab. The company plans to explore strategic partnerships and licensing agreements to secure non-dilutive financing. Management is also focused on addressing operational challenges by enhancing internal controls and information technology systems to prevent data breaches and ensure accurate financial reporting.

SEC Filing: CytoDyn Inc. [ CYDY ] - 10-K - Jul. 25, 2025

data.title: AMREP CORP. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
AMREP Corporation, primarily engaged in land development and homebuilding operations in New Mexico, has released its annual 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $49.7 million, reflecting a decrease of 3% compared to the previous year, primarily due to a decrease in land sale revenues and other revenues.
  • Operating Income: $12.1 million, an increase from $7.6 million in the prior year, driven by a reduction in total costs and expenses.
  • Net Income: $12.7 million, up from $6.7 million in the previous year, indicating improved profitability.
  • Earnings per share - diluted: $2.37, compared to $1.25 in the prior year, reflecting the increase in net income.

Business Highlights

  • Land Development Operations: AMREP Corporation owns approximately 16,600 acres in Sandoval County, New Mexico, focusing on selling both developed and undeveloped real property to various builders and developers, with significant activities in Rio Rancho, New Mexico.
  • Homebuilding Operations: The company operates a homebuilding segment in New Mexico, offering a variety of home floor plans and elevations. It focuses on single-family detached and attached homes, utilizing both built-to-order and speculative home sales strategies.
  • Sales Units: In 2025, the company sold 50 homes compared to 36 homes in 2024. The number of new construction single-family residential starts in Rio Rancho was 973 in 2025, slightly down from 1,007 in 2024.
  • Geographical Performance: The company conducts a substantial portion of its business in Rio Rancho, New Mexico, which is the third largest city in the state. The company has no foreign sales or activities outside the United States.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates a reduction in revenues from the sale of developed residential land during the fiscal year ending April 30, 2026, due to market headwinds and uncertainty, as well as an increase in entitlement and infrastructure delays.
  • Operational Challenges: The company experienced delays in municipal entitlements, infrastructure availability, and utility response times, impacting both land development and homebuilding segments. Rising costs of housing and mortgage interest rates have also affected demand.
  • Infrastructure Reimbursement Mechanisms: The company utilizes public improvement districts and private infrastructure reimbursement covenants to recover certain development costs, which have contributed to changes in land sale cost of revenues.
  • Human Capital Resources: As of April 30, 2025, the company employed 49 individuals, focusing on attracting and retaining talent through competitive pay and benefits. The company emphasizes ethical behavior and performance enhancement through technology and training.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Liquidity and Capital Resources: The company has focused on managing its liquidity and capital resources by maintaining a revolving line of credit with BOKF, NA dba Bank of Albuquerque, which allows for up to $5,750,000 in borrowings for general corporate purposes. Additionally, the company has engaged in equipment financing with Deere & Company to support its construction equipment needs.
  • Project Reductions: The company has strategically reduced the number and scope of its active land development projects due to market headwinds and uncertainty, which is expected to impact future revenues.
  • Capital Management: The company has managed its capital by maintaining a balance of cash, cash equivalents, and U.S. Government Securities, which increased by 32% from the previous year. The company has also utilized its cash flows from operations to support its working capital requirements and has reduced its notes payable from $35,000 to $26,000 through principal repayments. Shareholders' equity increased due to net income and stock compensation expenses, with no dividends declared or stock repurchases reported.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates that its primary demand for funds will continue to be for the development and acquisition of land, construction of home and commercial projects, and general administrative expenses. The company plans to explore public or private offerings of securities, asset disposals, or new debt facilities to meet its capital needs. The company also expects to face continued uncertainty in economic conditions, which may affect its ability to sell properties and generate cash flow from operations.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market and Operational Challenges: The company faces challenges related to the acquisition of new properties to sustain growth, as well as competition from other land developers and homebuilders. The development of properties requires financing, which may not always be available, posing a risk to future growth.
  • Regulatory and Compliance Risks: The company experienced delays in municipal entitlements, infrastructure availability, and utility response times, impacting construction timelines and increasing costs. Rising housing costs, interest rates, and general inflation have affected housing affordability, potentially reducing demand. The company has responded by offering sales incentives, reducing lot sizes, and leasing completed homes.
  • Economic Conditions: Future economic conditions remain uncertain, with potential impacts from changes in mortgage rates, inflation, and labor shortages. The company’s financial performance is sensitive to changes in market conditions, including the availability of financing for land development and homebuilding projects.
  • Revenue Impact: The company has noted a reduction in revenues from land sales due to market headwinds and entitlement delays, which may continue to affect future revenues.
  • Market Risk Exposure: Although the company is not required to provide quantitative and qualitative disclosures about market risk as a smaller reporting company, the management discussion highlights exposure to risks related to interest rate fluctuations and inflation, which could impact financing costs and operating margins.

SEC Filing: AMREP CORP. [ AXR ] - 10-K - Jul. 25, 2025
